name: Docker-Sponsored Open Source Program
description: The Docker-Sponsored Open Source (DSOS) Program provides verified badges, repository logo customization, insights and analytics, Docker Scout access, and removal of rate limiting for open-source projects.

perks.title: Verified Open Source Badge
perks.description: A Docker-Sponsored Open Source badge on Docker Hub, making it easier for users to identify trusted, secure, and active projects.

perks.title: Unlimited Pulls & Egress
perks.description: Removal of rate limiting for developers — program members and all users pulling public images from the project namespace get unlimited pulls and egress.

perks.title: Docker Scout Access
perks.description: Enable Docker Scout on up to 100 repositories for free, with automatic image analysis and supply chain management.

perks.title: Insights & Analytics
perks.description: Usage metrics showing image pulls by tag or digest, with breakdowns by geolocation, cloud provider, client, and more.

perks.title: Repository Logo Customization
perks.description: Upload custom logos for individual repositories on Docker Hub to override the default organization logo.

eligibility: Must share the project namespace in public repositories on Docker Hub.
Must meet the Open Source Initiative (OSI) definition of open source.
Must be in active development with no pathway to commercialization.
applicationProcess: Apply through the Docker-Sponsored Open Source Program application page.
Docker reviews the application for eligibility.
